steps.title: Identify the dangling modifier
steps.content: The opening phrase 'Working with equipment designed for a billionths of a meter scale' is a participial modifier — its subject must be whoever is doing the working: the nanoengineers, not the nanoparticles or possessive 'nanoengineers'.'

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: dangling modifier / misplaced modifier. The opening participial phrase "Working with equipment designed for a billionths of a meter scale" must be immediately followed by the noun doing that working. Rule: the subject of the main clause must be the agent of the opening participial phrase.
  • Choice A — nanoengineers have synthesized silica nanoparticles that are used to administer medicines to specific cells in the body.: Places "nanoengineers" right after the modifier — they are the ones working with the equipment. ✓
  • Choice B — nanoengineers' synthesized silica nanoparticles are used to administer medicines to specific cells in the body.: Uses a possessive; the subject of the clause is "nanoparticles," not "nanoengineers" — the nanoparticles are not doing the working. ✗
  • Choice C — silica nanoparticles that have been synthesized by nanoengineers are used to administer medicines to specific cells in the body.: Subject is "silica nanoparticles" — nanoparticles cannot work with equipment. ✗
  • Choice D — synthesized silica nanoparticles used to administer medicines to specific cells in the body have been created by nanoengineers.: Subject is "nanoparticles" again; the modifier dangles. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
